label: Surface splits ACTIVE (rename) vs IMMUTABLE specs history (exclude); naive global replace would falsify history — needs allowlist+denylist
Skill dir is dominant owner (~1063 refs/200+ files); git mv then sweep internal content (SKILL.md, feature_catalog, manual_testing_playbook, references, scripts, assets) + name: frontmatter
Four runtime agent mirrors (.opencode/.claude/.gemini/.codex) rename in lockstep + 3 README.txt + .codex/config.toml + root AGENTS.md/README.md

Skill-advisor graph is highest dangling risk: skill_advisor.py:39, skill-graph.json:5, regression fixtures:4, explicit.ts:16, aliases.ts:4 — regenerate via advisor_rebuild/skill_graph_scan, don't blind-edit
aliases.ts enables zero-downtime alias-bridge migration (old name resolves during transition; removed last)
Generated indexes (descriptions.json:57, graph-metadata.json, skill-graph.json, code-graph) must rebuild LAST after source; rebuilding early re-bakes old name

Scope fork: literal skill-id swap vs wider agent-improvement token family (start-agent-improvement-loop command, agent-improvement/ asset dirs, agent-improvement-state.jsonl) — wide touches resume/continuity
Cross-skill/runtime refs: deep-loop-runtime integration_points, sk-doc agent_template, cli-opencode family, root/install docs; sk-prompt-models link is INDIRECT via cli-opencode
test-fixtures/060-stress-test mirrors target cp-improve-target (a fixture), not the skill id — sweep references but do NOT rename cp-improve-target or stress tests break
Daemon mass-write hazard during index rebuild (1400+ graph-metadata files, unstaged); scope commits, never git add -A
Recommended 10-step alias-bridged ordering with validate gate (advisor_validate + skill_graph_validate + vitest + fresh route-check + residual census=0); atomic single-commit is the fallback
